name: control_phone
description: Do a task on the user's OWN phone using the Gemma AI that runs ON the phone itself — the BlackBox wakes it over Tailscale. THIS is the tool for 'do X on my phone', 'use my phone to ...', 'have my phone open/show/check ...'. It drives the phone's on-device Gemma, which is DIFFERENT from control_device (where a cloud model drives the phone instead). Only SAFE actions run remotely. It BLOCKS while the phone wakes Gemma (a cold model load, up to ~75s) — you MUST actually call it and WAIT for its result before you reply.

task.description: The natural-language task to run on the phone, e.g. 'open Google Maps and search for coffee near me' or 'what's on my screen right now?'.

returns: The result text reported by the on-device model, or a structured error (error_kind: no_device / wake_failed / refused / bad_response / lost_contact / remote_error / timeout).
notes: Capabilities: open apps, navigate the UI, read the screen, set a timer, show a map, run a web search, toggle the flashlight, take a photo. Recommended: tell the user 'Waking Gemma on your phone — I'll report back', then wait for the tool result. High-consequence actions (send SMS/email, place calls, payments) are refused remotely and must be done by the user directly. Frontier->phone inversion: the BlackBox reaches out to the originating operator's phone (resolved via the tailnet mesh) and drives its on-device Gemma. Blocking with wake-tracking; the phone enforces a remote allowlist. v1 targets the originating operator's device; Phase 2 adds an optional device param. See docs/plans/2026-06-18-frontier-to-phone-control.md.
